cross_scene_arcs.title: From Private Pain to Public Reckoning
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Debi transitions from internalizing the emotional shock of Martin's return to actively confronting him through public exposure, using her radio platform as a tool of empowerment and accountability.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Debi is emotionally disoriented and unprepared for Martin's sudden reappearance, seeking to articulate her pain.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She asserts control by broadcasting their conversation, publicly humiliating Martin and reclaiming narrative authority over their shared history.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Despite her performance of strength, Debi masks lingering vulnerability and unmet emotional needs, leaving her true closure uncertain.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Push and Pull of Reconnection
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Debi oscillates between emotional distance and growing intimacy with Martin, gradually opening to reconnection before ultimately stepping back into a private, vulnerable space with him—only for this fragile bond to be shattered by violence.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Debi remains guarded, challenging Martin's image of success while asserting her values of authenticity.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She initiates physical and emotional intimacy by leading Martin into the Nurse's Office, signaling a decisive shift toward re-engagement.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Her ambivalence persists—engaged yet skeptical, drawn in yet self-protective—leaving the authenticity and sustainability of their connection in question.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Confronting the Truth of Martin's Identity
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Debi moves from seeking emotional clarity about Martin to confronting the horrifying reality of his identity as a professional assassin, culminating in a moral and existential crisis that forces her to reject him despite her feelings.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Debi hopes Martin is a victim, not a perpetrator, clinging to a version of him that aligns with her moral worldview.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She fully rejects him after witnessing his violent actions and hearing his proposal, recognizing that love cannot justify complicity in his world of murder.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The emotional toll of loving someone irredeemable by her standards remains with her, unresolved and haunting.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Illusion of Safety and Control
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Debi's perceived control over her environment and emotional life erodes as Martin's violence intrudes into her reality, transforming her from a woman in command of her narrative to a target fighting for survival.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Debi appears in control, observing Martin from a distance while continuing her routine, unaware of his lethal intent.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She is now actively fleeing for her life, realizing that Martin's love is inseparable from danger and that her agency is compromised by forces beyond her comprehension.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether Debi can reclaim autonomy or will remain trapped in the orbit of Martin's violent world remains unknown.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Erosion of Detachment: From Isolation to Emotional Awakening
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Martin transitions from emotional numbness to confronting long-repressed feelings, primarily through his reconnection with Debi Newbery. This arc traces his journey from professional isolation to vulnerability, as memories, nostalgia, and desire disrupt his carefully maintained detachment.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Martin is emotionally detached, using work to suppress personal connections.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Martin openly confesses his enduring love for Debi, prioritizing emotional intimacy over mission focus.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Despite his emotional breakthrough, Martin remains unable to reconcile his violent identity with the possibility of authentic love.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Confronting the Past: The Collapse of Escape
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Martin's attempt to suppress his past is systematically dismantled by external triggers—his mother, childhood home, and high school reunion—forcing him to face unresolved trauma and identity dissonance. His avoidance strategies fail, culminating in public performance anxiety and emotional exposure.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Martin actively suppresses personal history, seeking control through emotional distance.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Martin is overwhelmed by the weight of his past, performing absurd lies to mask his inner emptiness at the reunion.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Martin's fear of being truly known prevents honest reconciliation, leaving him trapped between past and present selves.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Moral Reckoning: The Assassin's Identity Crisis
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Martin's professional identity is challenged by rival assassins, moral parallels with corporate violence, and his own growing disillusionment. He evolves from a detached contractor to a man who rejects his role, ultimately choosing protection over assassination.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Martin operates as a solitary, efficient hitman, emotionally disengaged from his work.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Martin abandons his mission, protects his target, and definitively breaks from the assassin code.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Martin's motivation for change—love, morality, or self-preservation—remains ambiguous, leaving his transformation emotionally complex and incomplete.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Double Life Unravels: Truth and Rejection
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Martin's attempt to reconnect with Debi is undermined by his secret identity. As he grows more emotionally open, the tension between truth and concealment escalates, culminating in his confession and her rejection—marking the collapse of his hope for redemption through love.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Martin seeks reconnection with Debi while hiding his true self, using irony and deflection.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Martin confesses his identity and is rejected as a 'monster,' left emotionally shattered and alone.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Debi's rejection leaves Martin without an anchor to a normal life, casting doubt on whether redemption is possible after such profound moral transgression.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Survival and Retribution: The Cycle of Violence Re-Engaged
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: After a failed assassination attempt, Martin is thrust back into survival mode. His brief respite from violence is shattered, and he shifts from passive target to active avenger, re-engaging with his lethal skills while attempting to impose moral limits on his actions.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Martin is caught off guard, nearly killed, and forced to react violently to survive.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Martin regains control, deciding to retaliate selectively—only against those who betrayed him—asserting agency within the cycle of violence.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Martin's desire for moral clarity clashes with his reliance on violence, leaving his ethical boundaries fragile and his future uncertain.
